title: Tangy Indian Lentil Sambhar
description: A creamy, one-pot Hyderabadi lentil stew bursting with tangy tamarind and aromatic spices, perfect for a halal-friendly meal.
instructions: Wash the red lentils until the water runs clear and set aside.
Heat the oil in the ceramic tagine over medium heat.
Add the mustard and cumin seeds, and let them splutter for 30 seconds.
Stir in the chopped onion and sauté until translucent about 5 minutes.
Add the ginger garlic paste and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Mix in the chopped tomatoes and cook until they soften, about 4 minutes.
Sprinkle the turmeric, red chili, and coriander powders, stirring well to coat the vegetables.
Pour in the vegetable broth and tamarind paste, bring the mixture to a gentle boil.
Add the rinsed lentils, lower the heat, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes or until lentils are tender.
Season with salt and simmer uncovered for 2 minutes.
Garnish with fresh cilantro before serving.
Serve hot with steamed rice or naan.
